entry.title: ChannelNewsAsia: 16 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Norwegian Hovland heads crowded Open leaderboard
  • Jeruto stuns steeplechase heats after champion Chepkoech withdraws
  • Biden ends trip with US-Saudi relations on the mend but few other wins
  • PSG sign striker Ekitike on loan from Reims
  • Pivac frustrated with Bok loss but happy with Wales progress
  • South Africa still have much to do says coach after series win over Wales
  • Two runners suspended for doping offences, ruled out of world championships
  • Hungarians rally against Orban's reforms, skeptical of change
  • Tour de lose as French riders look for elusive stage win
  • South Africa win third test against Wales to secure series
  • DeChambeau says St Andrews course can't be conquered
  • Kenya's Cherono, American Ross provisionally suspended, to miss worlds
  • Iran imposes sanctions on 61 Americans as nuclear talks hit impasse
  • Russia says it's stepping up operations as more rockets hit Ukraine
  • 2 imported cases of new Omicron subvariant BA.2.75 reported in Singapore
  • Trans woman Thomas nominated for NCAA Woman of the Year award
  • Beer for sunflower oil? Munich pub finds way to beat frying crunch
  • Gnabry offers Bayern good news by extending contract
  • Kane on target again as Tottenham draw 1-1 with Sevilla
  • Matthews fights back for Tour stage win, Vingegaard holds off Pogacar
  • G20 finance chiefs resolved on food security; Ukraine war prevents formal communique
  • Thousands take part in Seoul LGBT festival, protesters rally
  • Sri Lanka's political crisis: What happens next?
  • Sri Lanka's ousted president says he 'took all possible steps' to prevent crisis
  • Macao to extend city lockdown, casino closure until Jul 22
  • Cooperation among opposition parties to be explored without compromising principles: Anwar
  • G20 finance talks to end without joint communique, say officials
  • Sri Lanka parliament to begin process of electing new president
  • US Navy ship again sails near disputed South China Sea islands
  • US approves possible sale of military assistance to Taiwan
  • 5 of Bali’s best babi guling restaurants for the island’s famous spit-roasted pork dish
  • Commentary: Is China stumbling into its own mortgage crisis?
  • The king of kings: Penang growers top competition with durians from 40-year-old Black Thorn tree
  • Commentary: Shinzo Abe set Japan on the path it is on today
  • India's HDFC Bank's April-June profit rises 19% on year
  • Myanmar orders companies, banks to suspend foreign loan repayments: Reports
  • Japan govt to support trading firms' stakes in Sakhalin-2- sources
  • Amazon pauses work on six new US office buildings to weigh hybrid work needs
  • US needs US$3 billion more to remove Huawei, ZTE from US networks, regulator says
  • Elon Musk seeks to block Twitter request for expedited trial
  • A year after alleged murder of River Valley High student, school has more counsellors and wellness services hub
  • National network for mental health to pilot well-being circles in the community
  • SAF training in Brunei to progressively resume to pre-pandemic levels from August
  • ‘The magic never diminishes’: A Liverpool match through the eyes of their Singapore fans
  • A motorcycle accident left him with aphasia. He’s now fighting to regain his speech
  • IN FOCUS: Fighting food waste and saving money - a week as a freegan in Singapore
  • Osei-Nketia keeps New Zealand's 100m national record in the family
  • Britain's Lake out of World Championships due to COVID
  • Jones credits veterans for second tour triumph Down Under
  • Eagles carry Lowry into Open hunt before crash back to earth
  • Wales make late replacements against South Africa
  • 'We're better than that', says disappointed Wallabies coach
  • Saudi prince told Biden that US has made mistakes too, Saudi minister says
  • US lawyer who represented Khashoggi detained in UAE, rights group says
  • UK to hold emergency response meeting ahead of record heat
  • Yellen met with officials from Saudi, Australia, South Africa, Singapore
  • US, Saudi Arabia agree on stopping Iran getting nuclear weapons
  • UK summons Russian envoy over Briton's death in breakaway east Ukraine region
  • Commentary: Should you still go on holiday if you have COVID-19?
  • US State Department approves potential arms sales worth US$1.5 billion: Pentagon
  • Ukrainian city grieves 4-year-old girl after Russian missile attack
CNA: Breaking News, Singapore News, World and Asia

entry.title: Sydney Morning Herald: 16 Jul, 2022
entry.summary: Sydney Morning Herald: 16 Jul, 2022 published at 2022-07-16 19:17PM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Wallabies player ratings: How the men in gold fared in the third Test against England
  • Freaking out about interest rate rises? Here are 5 things to do today
  • ‘I thought I was the one to take us to the next level’: Dew’s belief in Suns
  • Tasmania’s got talent but is it enough? The AFL’s quest for 2026
  • Inside the Ukraine war crimes investigation, Part 2: Torture cells
  • Melbourne’s ‘red wall’ of shrinking suburbs revealed
  • ‘People aren’t following rules’: Plan to unleash dogs on Sydney beaches splits locals
  • ‘Dark, dingy and losing its character’: Can Chinatown’s decline be turned around?
  • ‘Crazy and highly selfish’: Police deployed to supervise private school drop off, pick up
  • Mortgages in retirement triple, outright ownership halves for most age groups
  • Victorians have gambled – and lost – $66 billion on pokies in 30 years
  • Boxing star Harry Garside: Trolling ‘affects me more’ than getting punched in the face
  • Paradise or purgatory? The Aussie billionaires buying islands
  • As equities plunge, could gold and cash be back on the menu?
  • ‘Major overhaul’: One-fifth of selective school places to go to disadvantaged students
  • Reason, religion and tax: should churches still be considered charities?
  • We fought the virus for more than two years, but now we must live with it
  • This unlikely British contender for PM is good news for Australia
  • On the fear of poetry - and the bounty we are denying our children
  • Netflix might have saved the royals, says Andrew Hastie

  • Best of cartoons, July 16, 2022

  • COVID-19 fine withdrawn in test case that could see thousands more torn up
  • Losing our religion and finding a church of love and respect
  • Three charged with murder over stabbing death of teen at Royal Easter Show
  • Why tens of thousands of bats have taken over Tamworth
  • One dead, another fighting for life after NSW house fires
  • Doctors demand end to ‘culture of cover up’ after string of patient deaths
  • War crimes in Ukraine - The Torture Cell
  • War crimes in Ukraine - The Missing
  • Mexico captures infamous drug lord Rafael Caro Quintero
  • Warning sirens sound in Kyiv as Russia steps up long-range bombardment
  • House votes to restore abortion rights, prohibit punishment
  • ‘He was’: Biden tells Saudi prince who was responsible for Khashoggi’s murder
  • Britons told to avoid alcohol, prepare for record ‘red alert’ heatwave
  • Spotlight back on church whose members were once known as ‘Moonies’
  • Why nobody wants to be in the office on Fridays
  • Ivana Trump died from ‘blunt force trauma injuries to her torso’
  • Australian Catholic Church ‘very open’ to elevating role of women
  • Target Time and Superquiz, Sunday, July 17
  • The Catholic Church should find ways to empower women
  • Measuring Melbourne’s mood as the city weathers another COVID winter
  • ‘I imagined she was one of the Hanson brothers’: Jonathan Van Ness on their first kiss
  • Mauricio Umansky arrives in Sydney without his Beverly Hills housewife
  • Stuck, sunk, bogged or buried? Aussie Salvage Squad has the answer
  • Is laughter really the best medicine?
  • Ab Fab? Not quite, but at least this comedy offers more than great music
  • Il Trovatore provides vividness, mystery, colour and pressing relevance
  • Decades after NWA fought off censorship, Australia has declared its own war on hip-hop
  • From Seth Meyers to Liam Gallagher, Amyl and the Sniffers soak up global spotlight
  • ‘It’s been unbelievable’: The surprising success of Everything Everywhere All at Once
  • Fed up with the chore wars, grumpy women are now politically dangerous
Breaking news from Sydney, Australia and the world. Features the latest business, sport, entertainment, travel, lifestyle, and technology news.

entry.title: Washington Post: 16 Jul, 2022
entry.content:

  • Golfers want to get paid, too
  • There is no authorization for cap-and-trade programs
  • D.C. should not offer traffic scofflaws a get-out-of-jail-free card

  • Chat with Perry Bacon Jr. about politics and beyond
  • Chat with Jennifer Rubin about her columns, politics, policy and more

Breaking news and analysis on politics, business, world national news, entertainment more. In-depth DC, Virginia, Maryland news coverage including traffic, weather, crime, education, restaurant reviews and more.

entry.title: Guardian: 16 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • The Open 2022: third round updates from St Andrews – live!

  • Finland v Germany: Women’s Euro 2022 – live!

  • Sri Lanka’s political crisis continues as Ranil Wickremesinghe bids to be president

  • Wales dream dashed as South Africa rediscover form in Cape Town decider

  • ‘Nudity on the beach is normal’: how Sardinia is tapping into the naturist revival

  • Johnson’s plan to give peerages to two MPs could leave successor facing early byelections

  • The right denied the story of a 10-year-old getting an abortion – it only gets worse
  • Marcus Smith’s breakaway sparks England to series victory over Australia
  • A million UK chickens ‘die needlessly each week to keep prices low’
  • Scalding oil, racist prank calls and endless ‘lid duty’: growing up in a Chinese restaurant
  • Paul Ryder brought funk to Happy Mondays’ debauched carnival of sound
  • You’re not taking the dog! How pet custody battles turned nasty
  • ‘Hi-tech’, underwhelming: Amazon’s IRL clothing store misses the point of shopping
  • McEnroe review – a deep dive into the psychology of a sporting phenomenon
  • Energy use from US cryptomining firms is contributing to rising utility bills
  • Ireland stun All Blacks again to make history with series triumph
  • Snoring was ruining my marriage – here’s how I finally cured myself
  • Reni Eddo-Lodge on anti-racism: ‘The backlash amazes me’
  • The Uber files lit up the darkness of big tech, and showed why we need whistleblowers | Frances Haugen
  • The women who wish they weren’t mothers: ‘An unwanted pregnancy lasts a lifetime’
  • No jobs, no money, no house: Ukrainians forced back to frontline towns
  • Brazil’s weak response to Phillips–Pereira murders threatens Indigenous people, lawyer says
  • At least six dead – including two children – after dust storm triggers mass pileup in Montana
  • House January 6 panel subpoenas Secret Service for allegedly deleted text messages
  • Daughter of doctor who gave 10-year-old an abortion faced kidnapping threat

Latest international news, sport and comment from the Guardian

entry.title: South China Morning Post: 16 Jul, 2022
entry.summary: South China Morning Post: 16 Jul, 2022 published at 2022-07-16 17:30PM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Hong Kong’s prosecutors will not bow to intimidation amid threat of US sanctions
  • Patriotic Education Centre to launch classes for Hong Kong students from September
  • Woman arrested for allegedly duping 22 residents out of HK$130,000 by offering fake discounted tickets to Hong Kong Disneyland
  • Macau extends coronavirus lockdown to next Friday, gives hard-hit businesses fresh round of financial aid
  • Hong Kong youth minister says she will not rule out bringing back ‘very progressive’ appointment system to pick district councillors
  • Hong Kong protests: 2 jailed fugitives among 12 caught at sea on Taiwan flee bid hit with extra 21 months in prison over petrol bombs, deadly items
  • Coronavirus: health chief to weigh experts’ call for Hong Kong to ease curbs and build natural immunity through infections; city logs 3,762 cases
  • New World First Bus fans vow to take photos and many rides before colours change to Citybus’ yellow and red following merger
  • London-based, anti-China group Friends of Hong Kong distances itself from members said to be involved in case of 4 protesters caught after 20 months in hiding
  • Time to ‘get away’: Hong Kong residents cooped up by Covid pandemic take flight as travel bug bites, never mind soaring airfares
  • Hong Kong phrase ‘add oil’ is older than you think – and we need it now more than ever
  • Enrolment slumps at Hong Kong’s direct subsidy scheme secondary schools, hit hardest by wave of emigration
  • Chinese foreign minister criticises US ‘meddling’ in Middle East as Joe Biden continues tour of region
  • Scientists urge people to take second Covid vaccine booster if offered as Omicron continues to spread
  • Why China, unlike Russia, won’t be drawn on civil unrest in Central Asia
  • More cities across China order partial Covid lockdowns
  • US approves possible sale of military assistance to Taiwan worth US$108 million
  • Chinese military upgrades near disputed Himalayan border viewed as provocative in India
  • ‘Too easy to borrow’: debt plagues young people despite China’s scrutiny of fintech microloans
  • China’s national carbon trading scheme marks one-year anniversary, with analysts expecting stricter regulation and data monitoring ahead
  • 5 unanswered questions about the Henan bank crisis
  • US-made HIMARS rockets are giving Ukraine an edge against Russia. Will Taiwan be next to benefit?
  • G20 in Indonesia agrees on food security, but war prevents formal communique
  • Malaysian assets at risk globally as heirs of sultan, who are Philippine nationals, claim US$15 billion award
  • 9 civilians shot dead in Indonesia’s restive Papua, days after protests over new law that will see 5 provinces, instead of current 2
  • Is Fiji’s ‘Little India’ facing extinction?
  • Sri Lanka economic crisis: presidential nominee Sajith Premadasa vows to catch those who looted nation
  • Is Singapore pursuing a false climate solution by buying Mekong hydropower?
  • Hundreds die as heatwave grips Europe and temperatures soar above 40ºC
  • US: 6 dead in Montana 21-car pile up after windy dust storm with ‘zero visibility’
  • ‘No vacuum for China, Russia, Iran’ to fill in Middle East, says Biden at Arab summit
  • Russia pounds Ukraine’s cities, kills more civilians, as it steps up operations
  • Thousands evacuated as blistering heatwave in Europe sparks wildfires
  • Unarmed black man Jayland Walker was shot 46 times by Ohio police, autopsy shows
  • Ukraine says Russia using Europe’s largest nuclear plant to store missiles
  • Mexico captures drug lord Rafael Caro Quintero who killed US agent
  • Elon Musk opposes Twitter’s bid to speed up trial over US$44 billion deal
  • Biden finds oil thicker than blood as he sets aside Saudi prince snub amid Ukraine war
  • UK PM contenders clash over taxes in contest to replace Boris Johnson
  • UK PM race: Downing Street cat Larry drawn into political fight for his residence
  • How HSBC laundered money for Mexican drug cartels, paid a US$1.9b fine and hardly anyone noticed
  • Technology is key to achieving Xi’s Jinping’s four goals for Hong Kong
  • ESG financiers need to first practise what they preach on climate change and net zero
  • WNBA players are the next big fashion icons, styled in Dior and streetwear for events and photographed in trending pregame outfits
  • Nothing Phone 1 review: iPhone lookalike is a good mid-range smartphone for a decent price, but not as great as the hype had us believe
  • This traditional Chinese medicine treatment boosts immunity against winter ills like colds, flu, allergies and respiratory illness
  • Thailand travel by train from Bangkok to Chiang Mai on the Northern Line offers visits to a series of former capitals – we hopped on board
  • Chris Patten’s Hong Kong Diaries of 1997 handover run-up take us behind the scenes as Britain prepared to take leave of its last colony
  • Anticipatory anxiety: what it is and how to overcome your fears, as cases rise with return to the office and going out after the pandemic
  • BTS’s J-Hope delves into his darker side with first solo album ‘Jack in the Box’
  • Indian ‘silver’ divorces rise, with couples more like ‘roommates than soul mates’
  • Meet Anastasia ‘Stassie’ Karanikolaou, Kylie Jenner’s best friend: she’s a fashion influencer who’s modelled for Rihanna’s Savage x Fenty … but what if that Kardashian fan event never happened?
  • Who is Huma Abedin, Bradley Cooper’s rumoured new romance? She’s Hillary Clinton’s right-hand woman, an author, and was married to disgraced US politician Anthony Weiner
  • 16 richest actors in Marvel’s Thor franchise – net worths, ranked: from Chris Hemsworth and Natalie Portman, of course, to Christian Bale and Scarlett Johansson, but who tops the list?
  • How Melania Trump is fighting back in 2022: the former US first lady has critiqued Joe Biden’s America, stood up to hubby Donald, and even taken a jab at Vogue’s Anna Wintour
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.

entry.title: JoongAng Daily: 16 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Tottenham 1-1 Sevilla — Spurs end Korea tour with draw in Suwon

  • 'Anyone for tennis equipment and apparel?' Korea asks
  • [WHY] No reservation needed at Korea's hottest dining spot — the convenience store
  • Making Itaewon: The evolution of Seoul's most diverse neighborhood
The Korea JoongAng Daily is an English-language daily published by the JoongAng Group, Korea’s leading media group, in association with The New York Times.

entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Japan sets a new daily record for COVID-19 cases; tally at 110,675

The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.
